name: Warm Terrine of Sausage
recipeIngredient: 1 large red bell pepper, cut into half-inch strips
1 large green bell pepper, cut into half-inch strips
1/2 pound fresh mozzarella, cubed (3 inches x 1/4 inch x 1/4 inch)
1 terrine dish (12 inch long by 4-inch wide by 4-inch deep)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bake the sausage for 20 minutes at 350 degrees Fahrenheit, then drain.
2. Crumble the cooked sausage to resemble rough bread crumbs and set aside.
3. In a medium saute pan, heat olive oil over low heat.
4. Gently saute garlic cloves until golden brown on all sides (about 8-10 minutes).
5. Add peppers and continue sauteing until soft but not browned (about 7-8 more minutes). Remove from heat.
6. Separate and let cool.
7. Bring mozzarella to room temperature; place sausage, peppers, garlic, and mozzarella in separate bowls.
8. In a large pot, bring 6 cups water to boil with salt and sugar dissolved.
9. Slowly add cornmeal to boiling water while whisking continuously.
10. Lower heat and cook until polenta is smooth and resembles hot cereal (about 30 minutes).
11. Remove from heat; quickly pour polenta into terrine mold about three-quarters full.
12. Sprinkle crumbled sausage over the polenta, then cover with warm polenta (1-1/2 cups).
13. Using a spatula, flatten and smooth the polenta to create a flat surface.
14. Layer peppers and garlic cloves; repeat with another layer of warm polenta (1-1/2 cups) until edges are reached.
15. Cover mozzarella over the polenta.
16. Pour remaining warm polenta into mold to fill terrine, smoothing the top.
17. Refrigerate overnight.
18. To serve, invert the terrine onto a cutting board; slice as bread, about 3/4 inch thick.
19. Place slices on baking sheet and bake for 10-12 minutes.
20. Sprinkle with grated cheese and serve immediately.
Yield: 6 appetizers
